title: ST558 Project 1 - Summer 2026
text:
Just getting started.

library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(curl)
library(dplyr)

key <- "70c77fa9cc244f5a9fb3c46ed731835d132c0670"

Let’s do a quick test!

url <- paste0("https://api.census.gov/data/2022/acs/acs1/pums?get=SEX,PWGTP,MAR&SCHL=24&key=", key)

response <- GET(url, timeout(120))
parsed <- fromJSON(rawToChar(response$content))
data <- as_tibble(parsed[-1, ])

Warning: The `x` argument of `as_tibble.matrix()` must have unique column names if
`.name_repair` is omitted as of tibble 2.0.0.
ℹ Using compatibility `.name_repair`.

colnames(data) <- parsed[1, ]

str(data)

tibble [44,079 × 4] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
 $ SEX  : chr [1:44079] "2" "2" "1" "1" ...
 $ PWGTP: chr [1:44079] "6" "23" "23" "80" ...
 $ MAR  : chr [1:44079] "5" "2" "3" "5" ...
 $ SCHL : chr [1:44079] "24" "24" "24" "24" ...


Now, these should not be character columns. We will fix that later if/when needed once we get our function running! Let’s build that function.

helper_fun <- function(year = 2022, 
                       num_vars = c("AGEP", "PWGTP"), 
                       cat_vars = c("SEX")) {
  if (year > 2022 || year < 2010) {
    stop("Year must be between 2010 and 2022, inclusive.")
  }
  if (!("PWGTP" %in% num_vars)) {
    num_vars <- c(num_vars, "PWGTP")
  }
  for (k in num_vars) {
    if (!(k %in% c("AGEP", "GASP", "GRPIP", "JWAP", "JWDP", "JWMNP", "PWGTP"))) {
      stop("Please choose only valid options for num_vars.")
    }
  }
  for (k in cat_vars) {
    if (!(k %in% c("FER", "HHL", "SCH", "SCHL", "SEX"))) {
      stop("Please choose only valid options for cat_vars.")
    }
  }
  print(year)
  print(num_vars)
  print(cat_vars)
}

helper_fun(2021)

[1] 2021
[1] "AGEP"  "PWGTP"
[1] "SEX"
